Spatially-derived fibroblast signatures onto scRNA-seq-data

Kürşat Birgin
Meeting, 13.01.2026

‹#›

Instead of:

If TSI score > Stroma score → assign "TSI-like”

Else → assign "Stroma-like”

I used (thanks to Anna V.):

# Min-max rescale rescale01 <- function(x) {   (x - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x)) }

to rescale, so the lowest score becomes 0, the highest becomes 1, and all other values are proportionally scaled in between.

Full scale

Top 75%

Full scale

Full scale

Nika

Calculation for Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)

Raw enrichment score:

Average log2 fold change of each gene between TSI_high and Stroma_high is calculated
Genes are ranked by how much they are higher in TSI_high versus Stroma_high 
GSEA checks whether genes from each pathway tend to appear near the top or bottom of this list 
A running sum over the ranked genes gives the raw enrichment score (ES)

Random permutations: To know whether the observed clustering is unusual, GSEA shuffles the ranked list many times and recalculates the enrichment score for each shuffle. This creates a “null distribution” of scores we would expect by chance.

Normalization: The raw score is then divided by the average of these random scores (or scaled based on the null distribution) to account for differences in pathway size (big pathways naturally get higher raw scores) and the overall distribution of the ranked genes.

Also: NES direction shows which group the pathway is enriched in (Positive NES → enriched in TSI_high / Negative NES → enriched in Stroma_high), and its magnitude reflects how strongly and consistently the genes shift in that direction.

Thank you all!☺️

‹#›
